questions.q: In what year did the United States enter World War I?
questions.explanation: The U.S. entered on the side of the Allies in 1917.

questions.q: What event began the Great Depression?
options: The end of World War I
questions.explanation: The 1929 crash began the worst economic crisis in U.S. history.

questions.q: What were President Franklin D. Roosevelt's programs to fight the Depression called?
questions.explanation: The New Deal provided relief, jobs, and reform, including Social Security.

questions.q: What event brought the United States into World War II?
options: The attack on Pearl Harbor in 1941
The fall of the Berlin Wall
questions.explanation: Japan's December 7, 1941 attack drew the U.S. into the war.

questions.q: By 1945, the United States had become what?
options: One of the most powerful nations in the world
questions.explanation: The war made the U.S. a leading global power.
